otherRegistrablePersonInformation.title: What is an other registrable person?
otherRegistrablePersonInformation.line1: An other registrable person (ORP) is a non-corporate entity or office that can legally act as a PSC. This can be:
otherRegistrablePersonInformation.bullet1: a corporation sole - a single incorporated office occupied by one person, such as Lord Mayor of London
otherRegistrablePersonInformation.bullet2: a government or government department of a country, a territory, or a part of a country or territory
otherRegistrablePersonInformation.bullet3: an international organisation whose members include two or more countries or territories (or their governments)
otherRegistrablePersonInformation.bullet4: a local authority or local government body in the UK or elsewhere

tellUsAboutPscPage.title: Tell us about people with significant control (PSCs)
tellUsAboutPscPage.pageInformation: We'll ask you whether the partnership will have any PSCs. If it will, you'll need to tell us about them.
tellUsAboutPscPage.moreThanOne: If the partnership has more than one PSC, you'll add them one at a time.
tellUsAboutPscPage.futureChanges: You'll need to file any future changes to PSCs on paper.

willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.title: Will the partnership have any people with significant control (PSCs) on registration?
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.optionYes: Yes, at least one person will count as a PSC in relation to the partnership. This may be an individual, a relevant legal entity (RLE) or an other registrable person (ORP)
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.optionNo: No, there is no person identified as a PSC in relation to the limited partnership
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.errorMessage: Select if the partnership will have any PSCs on registration
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.publicRegisterLine1: We'll show this information on the public register.

commonEntityFields.publicRegisterLine1: We'll show all of this information on the public register.
